page: Amazon Web Services gr6f.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 16 dedicated vCPUs derived from 8 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The hardware configuration includes a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU providing 11 GB of VRAM, paired with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz. Local storage is provided via a 450 GB NVMe SSD, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 10 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, while multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench scores fall within the average range. Large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth benchmarks are weak, landing in the bottom 25%. However, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in the top 10% for LLM inference prompt processing using small and medium models, and strong performance in the top 25% for prompt processing with large models. Text generation speeds for these models remain in the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio offers a high memory allocation relative to compute cores, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring moderate GPU acceleration alongside substantial memory capacity. The combination of dedicated AMD EPYC cores, an NVMe SSD, and a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU makes this instance appropriate for graphics-intensive tasks, small-to-medium LLM inference, database operations, and memory-intensive applications that do not require a full dedicated GPU.
description: Amazon Web Services gr6f.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual server utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU with 11 GB of VRAM. Storage is supported by a local 450 GB NVMe SSD, and network throughput baseline is 10 Gbps. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier LLM inference prompt processing speeds, though uncached large-block memory bandwidth is weak. With its 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio, this instance provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads requiring moderate GPU acceleration. It is suited for graphics rendering, database operations, and small-to-medium language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services gr6f.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual server featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU with 11 GB VRAM, and a 450 GB NVMe SSD.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services gr6f.4xlarge is a virtual server with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU, and 450 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An x86 virtual server combining a fractional graphics accelerator with a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB of DDR4 memory
Fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU with 11 GB of VRAM
450 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier benchmark performance for LLM inference prompt processing
Appropriate for graphics-intensive tasks, database operations, and memory-heavy workloads
